answer:
**Āṅgirasa Smṛti, Chapter 1**

The lotus-born creator (Brahmā) placed all the divine śrāddha-vegetables on one scale-pan and the jackfruit (*panasa*) on the other, weighed them with his hand, and found the jackfruit slightly heavier: "Placing all these in one scale-pan, the lotus-born (Brahmā), placing the *panasa* (पनस) in the other pan, weighed them with his hand. // Ang_1.531 //"[^1] "Then the *panasa* (पनस) became slightly heavier."[^2]

On that same balance, the eggplant (*bṛhatī*) was equal to three hundred, ginger (*ārdraka*) to six hundred, and sesame (*tila*) to one hundred vegetables: "The *bṛhatī* (बृहती, eggplant) then became equal to three hundred, in the sight of all. // Ang_1.532 //"[^3] "Ginger (*ārdraka*) was equal to six hundred, and sesame (*tila*) was equal to one hundred. Thus, this trio was established on the balance scale from that day onwards. // Ang_1.533 //"[^4]

[^1]: Āṅgirasa Smṛti, [Chapter 1, paragraph p-4fe9a6](/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-4fe9a6).

[^2]: Āṅgirasa Smṛti, [Chapter 1, paragraph p-97de7d](/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-97de7d).

[^3]: Āṅgirasa Smṛti, [Chapter 1, paragraph p-675fe0](/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-675fe0).

[^4]: Āṅgirasa Smṛti, [Chapter 1, paragraph p-104051](/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-104051).

args.query: "eggplant" OR bṛhatī OR "ginger" OR ārdraka OR "sesame" OR tila śrāddha
